name: Chicken & Andouille Sausage Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
Prepare seasoning mix. Remove skin from the 8 boneless chicken breasts and cut them into bite-sized pieces. Place the pieces on a baking sheet and sprinkle with the seasoning, letting stand at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Place flour in a plastic bag; add seasoned chicken, shaking to coat all pieces thoroughly. Remove the chicken; shake it in a colander to remove all excess flour, reserving the flour.
Heat 1 cup lard in a large soup pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat until very hot. Add chicken in batches and stir until browned and crisp on all sides. Remove from heat. Using a slotted spoon, remove the chicken and set aside.
Loosen any browned bits from the bottom of the pan; strain fat to remove particles. Return the fat to the pot over medium-low heat.
Add reserved flour; whisk until a smooth mahogany-colored roux is formed, about 45 minutes. Remove from heat; add onion, bell pepper, and celery at once, stirring to blend and prevent browning. Cook until vegetables are wilted and onions are transparent.
Slowly stir in stock, stirring until combined before adding more. When all the stock has been added, bring to a full boil. Reduce heat; add sausage, garlic, salt, black pepper, and cayenne. Add browned chicken; simmer 25 minutes, stirring often. Add okra; cook for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove from heat; stir in green onions and parsley. To serve, place half a cup of rice in each soup plate. Spoon gumbo over the rice. Makes 8 to 10 servings.
